_comment: Volatile extraction patterns for YouTube's obfuscated player base.js, ported from yt-dlp (Unlicense / public domain). The sig/nsig function NAMES and the global-array prelude shape change with every player build; keeping the regexes here (embedded via include_str! in cipher.rs, NOT hardcoded in source) means the weekly canary (scripts/yt-canary.*) refreshes them as a data edit. Each pattern documents its yt-dlp origin and what it matches. Patterns are Rust `regex` crate syntax: named groups use (?P<name>...), and we DO NOT use Python's (?P=name) backreferences (the regex crate has no backrefs) — where yt-dlp relied on a backref we substitute a tolerant character class, which is safe because we re-validate the captured name against the function body before use.

player_js_url._origin: yt-dlp youtube/_video.py _extract_player_url / _player_url; the watch page and config embed the player path.
player_js_url._matches: The /s/player/<id>/.../base.js path inside the watch page HTML or ytcfg (jsUrl / PLAYER_JS_URL).

player_id._origin: yt-dlp youtube/_video.py _extract_player_info: the 8-hex player id in the player URL path.
player_id._matches: The player revision id, e.g. /s/player/ac678d18/player_ias.vflset/en_US/base.js -> ac678d18. Used as the cache key for the per-player extracted functions.

signature_cipher_response._origin: yt-dlp youtube/_video.py: web formats carry `signatureCipher` (urlencoded s=..&sp=..&url=..) instead of a direct `url`.
signature_cipher_response._matches: ytInitialPlayerResponse JSON field name; we read it out of the web player response in player_js.rs, this is documentation only.

sig_function_name._origin: yt-dlp youtube/_video.py _parse_sig_js (rev 2025.03.31). Names the top-level signature-descramble function. Ordered most-current -> oldest; first match wins.
sig_function_name._matches: The function applied to decodeURIComponent(s) before .set(...): e.g. `Xva&&(Xva=sig(decodeURIComponent(Xva)))` or `sig=function(a){a=a.split("");...;return a.join("")}`.

sig_function_body._origin: yt-dlp jsinterp.py JSInterpreter.extract_function_code. Given the name, captures `function(args){ <code> }` (any of the 3 declaration forms). We then brace-balance the body in Rust because regex can't match nested braces.
sig_function_body._matches: The signature function's argument list + opening brace; the body is taken by balanced-brace scan from the captured `{`.

helper_object._origin: yt-dlp jsinterp.py JSInterpreter.extract_object. The signature body calls a small helper object's methods (swap/reverse/splice). We capture `var OBJ={ ... };` by name, then balanced-brace the object literal.
helper_object._matches: The transform object, e.g. `var Mr={Gk:function(a){a.reverse()},...};`. Its name is the first `IDENT.method(` token in the sig body.

nsig_function_name._origin: yt-dlp youtube/_video.py _extract_n_function_name (rev 2025.03.31). Names the `n`-throttle function, including the global-array indirection (nfunc[idx]) and the `"nn"[+x]` / String.fromCharCode(110) obfuscation of the literal "n".
nsig_function_name._matches: Examples: `.get("n"))&&(b=nfunc(b)`; `.get("n"))&&(b=narray[idx](b)`; `b=String.fromCharCode(110),c=a.get(b))&&(c=narray[idx](c)`; `a.D&&(b="nn"[+a.D],c=a.get(b))&&(c=narray[idx](c)...`. Group `nfunc` is the name (or array name when `idx` is present).
nsig_function_name._fallback_note: yt-dlp's fallback used a `\}(?!;)` lookahead to stop at the function's closing brace; the Rust `regex` crate has NO lookahead, so we approximate with a lazy any-char run up to the `_w8_` return sentinel. This is a best-effort last resort; the primary pattern is what normally matches.

array_deref._comment: When the name match yields nfunc + idx, the real function name is element [idx] of `var nfunc=[...]`. We capture the array literal by name then JSON-ish parse it.

player_js_global_var._origin: yt-dlp youtube/_video.py _extract_player_js_global_var (rev 2025.03.31). The big `'use strict'; var X="a,b,c".split(",")` (or `=[..]`) global array the sig/nsig functions index into; must be prepended as the prelude when running nsig.
player_js_global_var._matches: `"use strict";var dwq="...".split("...")` or `var dwq=["..","..",..]`. Group `code` is the whole `var X=...` statement (the prelude), `name` is X.
